title: Simple TCA/acetone protein extraction protocol for proteomics studies.
author: Angelo José Rinaldi

steps.title: Prepare a 20% TCA solution by...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 20% TCA solution by adding 200g of TCA in 800ml of distilled water. Be sure to do this in a well-ventilated area, wearing gloves and eye protection, as TCA is corrosive.

steps.title: Add the biological sample (approximately 300...
steps.instruction: Add the biological sample (approximately 300 mg) to a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add a small amount of detergent...
steps.instruction: Add a small amount of detergent (10% SDS) to the sample, if necessary, to break down cell membranes and facilitate protein release. The exact amount of detergent depends on the sample type and must be optimized experimentally.

steps.title: Transfer the homogenized sample to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenized sample to a new microcentrifuge tube and centrifuge at high speed (eg 13,000 rpm) at 4°C for 20 minutes to remove cell debris and nuclei.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant carefully, without disturbing...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant carefully, without disturbing the pellet formed at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Add concentrated TCA (20% final) to...
steps.instruction: Add concentrated TCA (20% final) to the pellet containing the proteins, in a ratio of 4 parts of TCA to 1 part of the sample. For example, if you have 200 µl of sample, add 800 µl of TCA.

steps.title: Incubate the sample on ice for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample on ice for 20-30 minutes to allow protein precipitation.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 4°C for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 4°C for 20 minutes at high speed (eg 13,000 rpm).

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant and add...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant and add 1 ml of 80% acetone (cooled to -20°C) to the pellet. Make sure the acetone completely covers the pellet. Note: Wash the pellet with 80% acetone, 2x. To do this, simply add 80% acetone and quickly stir using a vortex tube stirrer and centrifuge at 13,000 RPM for 5 min.

steps.title: Incubate the sample on ice for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample on ice for 5 minutes to allow for further protein precipitation.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 4°C for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 4°C for 20 minutes at high speed (eg 13,000 rpm).

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant and dry...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant and dry the protein pellet at room temperature.

steps.title: The dried protein pellet can be...
steps.instruction: The dried protein pellet can be resuspended in an extraction buffer (10% SDS with 6.8 tris-HCl) suitable for further analysis or apply 10 µL onto the SDS-PAGE electrophoresis gel.
